Tests specific functionality related to switch and while statements in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates basic control flow structures in JavaScript. It defines an empty function [`f`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-669f>) that does not perform any operations. The `switch` statement evaluates the expression `1` and matches it with the `case 1`, executing the `break` statement to exit the switch block. The `while` loop is set to execute while the condition is `false`, which means the loop body containing the `continue` statement will never execute. This script provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as an example of syntax for function declaration, switch-case, and while loop constructs.
